channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Karen Read, accused killer of cop boyfriend, showed no emotion until jury deliberations: documentary
item.description: Massachusetts woman Karen Read is accused of striking her Boston Police officer boyfriend John O'Keefe with her SUV and leaving him to die in a snowstorm.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tornado threat moves south after central states hit by massive storm
item.description: The threat of tornadoes moved east into the Mississippi Valley and the south on Saturday, just a day after a massive storm in several central states.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Monster storm across the US sparks threat of tornadoes in US South; Texas dust storm claims 3 lives
item.description: The threat of tornadoes is moving east into the Mississippi Valley and Deep South a day after a massive storm system moving across the country unleashed winds that damaged buildings, whipped up dust storms that caused deadly crashes and fanned more than 100 wildfires in several central states.
item.dc:creator: Sean Murphy, Russ Bynum And Hallie Golden, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Extreme weather expected Saturday gets rarely used 'high risk' designation
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A dynamic storm system threatening to spawn powerful tornadoes and hail as big as baseballs has earned a relatively rare designation from forecasters: A “high risk” day of severe weather.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Appeals court says Louisiana can carry out the state’s first nitrogen gas execution next week
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Louisiana's first execution using nitrogen gas is set to move forward as planned next week after a federal appeals court on Friday vacated a preliminary injunction granted by a lower judge. With a March 18 date hastily nearing, attorneys for Jessie Hoffman Jr.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Oklahoma man says Social Security benefits terminated without warning or explanation
item.description: A local retiree says his Social Security benefits were suddenly suspended without warning — and with no explanation given when he reached out. He worries it may have to do with the place he was born, and ongoing DOGE cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, KRON4

item.title: Trump pledges to 'expose' his enemies in political speech at Justice Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump pledged to “expose” his enemies during a norm-breaking political speech Friday at the Justice Department in which he aired a litany of grievances about the criminal cases he faced and vowed retribution for what he described as the “lies and abuses that have o
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: More than 50 universities face federal investigations as part of Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 50 universities are being investigated for alleged racial discrimination as part of President Donald Trump’s campaign to end diversity, equity and inclusion programs that his officials say exclude white and Asian American students.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California man out on bond for attempted murder charged with kidnapping 89-year-old South Carolina woman
item.description: A California man reportedly out on bond for an attempted murder is charged with kidnapping an elderly woman in South Carolina over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Viral 'American Idol' contestant dead at 32, family says
item.description: Douglas Kiker, the garbage collector who touched hearts with his singing voice on "American Idol" has died, his family announced on social media. He was 32 years old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here's what you need to know about St. Patrick's Day
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If it's March, and it's green, it must be St. Patrick's Day. The day honoring the patron saint of Ireland is a global celebration of Irish heritage.
item.dc:creator: DEEPTI HAJELA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Voice of America ending contracts with Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France Presse
item.description: The government agency that runs the Voice of America has moved to terminate contracts with The Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France-Presse, and told its journalists Friday to stop using material from the wire services.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 12 taken to hospital with minor injuries after plane catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Southwest Airlines slammed as 'tone deaf' for social media joke
item.description: Let the lesson be learned, social media managers: There is a time and place for jokes. This apparently was not one of those times. Southwest Airlines has been catching heat for its recent policy reversal.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Where you live can change your chances of getting dementia: Study
item.description: The study shows that air pollution and access to green spaces can affect your odds of developing dementia.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: Arrest made after crash kills at least 5 people, including infant, in Texas
item.description: At least five people died and 11 were hurt in a crash involving at least 17 vehicles, including semi-trucks, on Interstate 35 in north Austin late Thursday night, police said.
item.dc:creator: Valentina Misas, Fox 5

item.title: Hundreds of federal offices may start shutting down this summer due to DOGE
item.description: Federal agencies will begin to vacate hundreds of offices across the country this summer under a frenetic and error-riddled push by Elon Musk’s budget-cutting advisers to terminate leases that they say waste money.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY, JOSHUA GOODMAN and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: DHS agents search Columbia dorm rooms after Khalil arrest
item.description: Department of Homeland Security agents searched two dorm rooms at Columbia University Thursday night, the school said, a move that comes less than a week after the federal arrest of former grad student Mahmoud Khalil.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: International Delight coffee creamer shipped to 31 states is recalled
item.description: Danone, the distributor, issued a voluntary recall on some International Delight coffee creamers on Feb. 21 following reports of potential spoilage and illness related to the products' use.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KRON4

item.title: Tesla says it is 'exposed' to retaliatory tariffs
item.description: Tesla warned U.S. trade representative (USTR) Jamieson Greer that the electric vehicle company and other American exporters are “inherently exposed” to retaliatory tariffs levied in response to ones from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WGN 9

item.title: Aircraft catches fire after landing in Denver, sending passengers onto wing as smoke engulfs plane
item.description: A fire on an American Airlines plane after it diverted mid-flight and landed at Denver International Airport sent fleeing passengers to a wing amid billowing smoke.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Peipert, Sarah Brumfield, Jesse Bedayn And Coleeen Slevin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 60 percent of voters unhappy with DOGE handling of federal workers: Poll
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE have been trying to slash their way through the federal government, offering buyouts to and laying off scores of workers. Many of their actions are now the subject of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Congress poised to force $1B cut to local DC budget, surprising many lawmakers
item.description: Congress is poised to pass a President Trump-endorsed funding bill this week that Washington, D.C., officials warn would lead to a $1 billion cut to the District’s local budget, a move that has been catching members of both parties off guard.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Burn pit fund for veterans on chopping block in GOP spending bill
item.description: The fund was meant to allot $22.8 billion to cover expanded benefits for former service members sickened by military toxic exposures — including burn pit smoke and Agent Orange water contamination.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump demands unprecedented control at Columbia, alarming scholars and speech groups
item.description: The Trump administration has delivered an extraordinary ultimatum to Columbia University, threatening to permanently end federal funding to the school unless it cedes control of an international studies department and implements sweeping changes to other policies.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione's defense fund raises over $722K
item.description: "People are raising money not for his innocence but because they're in support of allegedly what he did.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, ABC 4

item.title: Second judge blocks mass firings of probationary federal workers
item.description: A second federal judge has blocked the Trump administration from firing federal workers, issuing an order Thursday that probationary employees be reinstated weeks after their dismissal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 12 taken to hospital after fire seen under American Airlines plane at DIA
item.description: Passengers were evacuated Thursday evening from an American Airlines flight after a fire was reported.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Columbia University punishes students who took over building during anti-Israel protests
item.description: Columbia University on Thursday announced a range of punishments, including expulsions and suspensions, for students who took over of a campus building last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Columbia University says it expelled some students who seized building last year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Columbia University has expelled or suspended some students who took over a campus building during pro-Palestinian protests last spring and temporarily revoked the diplomas of others who have since graduated, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls 61K sparkling water bottles
item.description: Trader Joe's announced a recall of two lots of the popular Gerolsteiner Sparkling Natural Mineral Water, a non-Trader Joe's branded item, which the grocery giant sells.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KRON4

item.title: Parents arrested after refusing child a ride to school; found walking on highway
item.description: The Midland Police Department arrested two people earlier this month after a young girl was found walking alone on a busy street in the early morning hours.
item.dc:creator: Gabriella Meza, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democratic-led states sue to block Trump administration layoffs at the Education Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A coalition of Democratic-led states is challenging the Trump administration’s sweeping layoffs across the Education Department, saying it amounts to an illegal dismantling of an agency created by Congress.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dem AGs sue over Trump administration’s Education Department layoffs
item.description: Democratic attorneys general in Washington, D.C., and 20 states sued Thursday over the Trump administration’s efforts to lay off nearly half of the Education Department workforce.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Immigrants fuel growth in major US urban counties
item.description: Immigrants kept the largest urban counties in the U.S. growing last year. Core counties in the Houston, Miami and Phoenix metropolitan areas grew more than any others in the country primarily because of people moving in from outside the United States, according to population estimates from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LA woman reunited with cat presumed lost in devastating Palisades Fire
item.description: A California woman who lost her home in the devastating firestorm that engulfed the Palisades experienced a tearful reunion over the weekend with her beloved cat, lost in the charred remains of the neighborhood for some two months.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Indonesian fishermen sue Bumble Bee and say the canned tuna giant knew of abuse in its supply chain
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Lawyers representing four Indonesian fishermen who say they were beaten and trapped on vessels that were part of the global supply chain that provided tuna to Bumble Bee Seafoods filed a lawsuit Wednesday against the canned seafood giant.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, ABC 4
